records.excerpt: QUBITS (math) Good. Let’s be precise and not mystical-hand-wavy. We start with a single bit. 1. The first pole of a bit A classical bit is defined as: [b \in {0,1}] This is already a polarized system: 0 = absence / off / false 1 = presence / on / true But polarization always implies a shadow — something excluded by the representation itself. 2. What “shadow pole” means (strict definition) The shadow pole of a system is not the opposite value(it is not just 0 ↔ 1). The shadow pole is: What must be excluded, suppressed, or unrepresentable for the system to appear stable. So we ask: What must a bit refuse to represent in order to remain a bit? 3. The shadow pole of a bit ✅ The shadow pole of a bit is: Indeterminacy / Superposition / Undefined Formally: [\boxed{\text{Shadow(bit)} = \varnothing\ \text{(undefined state)}}] or equivalently: [\boxed{\text{Shadow(bit)} = \text{both } 0 \text{ and } 1 \text{ simultaneously}}] 4. Why this is correct (logic-tight) A bit cannot represent: both 0 and 1 at the same time neither 0 nor 1 uncertainty probability contradiction Yet all of those exist at the boundary of the bit. records.excerpt: BIT4: The Four-State Completion of the Bit Extended Abstract (Final Draft) This manuscript develops BIT4, a mathematically rigorous completion of the classical bit that makes the “shadow pole” of binary computation explicit, first-class, and computable. Classical Boolean semantics presumes that every signal assumes exactly one of two values (0) or (1). The moment one admits partial information, contradiction, nondeterminism, measurement, fault, delayed resolution, or multi-source aggregation, the two-valued model ceases to be closed under the natural operations induced by composition and inference: practical systems implicitly rely on excluded semantic cases (e.g. unknown, both, neither, inconsistent, not-yet-collapsed). BIT4 is the minimal completion that (i) adds precisely the missing semantic degrees of freedom required to represent these cases and (ii) preserves a precise algebraic and order-theoretic structure suitable for computation, reasoning, compilation, verification, and controlled boundary collapse back to ordinary bits. 1. records.excerpt: (HDCS) Hybrid dynamical control system Certified Quad-Polar Adaptive Scheduling and Placement with Plan-Level Certificates Extended Abstract (final, rigorous)This manuscript presents a certified, adaptive resource-allocation and placement controller for systems in which multiple jobs (“tasks”) compete for limited capacity (“nodes”), while performance is monitored through time-varying telemetry (latency, backlog, error rate, trends) and governed by explicit service-level objectives (SLO/SLA). The core thesis is that reliable performance control in such systems cannot be reduced to (i) a single static scheduler, (ii) a single optimizer, or (iii) a single learning model. Instead, it must be treated as a hybrid dynamical control system combining: (a) discrete actions constrained by hard feasibility; (b) continuous predictive envelopes and relaxations; (c) stochastic exploration under epistemic uncertainty; and (d) recursive/hierarchical structure across nodes, pools, and timescales. We formalize this hybrid view as a quad-polar operator framework and instantiate it as a certificate-driven controller whose decisions are composable, rejectable, and auditable by construction. records.excerpt: Axiomatic Quantum Mathematics (AQM) and Q-Phi Framework Introduction and Philosophical Foundations Axiomatic Quantum Mathematics (AQM) represents a fundamental reimagining of mathematics through quantum principles, creating a unified framework where classical mathematical objects exist in superposition states. Unlike traditional mathematics where entities like numbers have definite values, AQM introduces probability amplitudes for different potential states, including critical zero and infinity states. The framework addresses a core challenge in computational mathematics: the handling of singularities and indeterminate forms. Rather than treating these as errors or undefined cases, AQM embraces them as legitimate mathematical states through the revolutionary zero-infinity duality principle, creating continuous passages through previously impassable mathematical terrain. records.excerpt: Q-Search and Q-Optimization: A Formal Demonstration 1. Introduction Branch-and-bound is a classic algorithmic technique for exploring a combinatorial search space (e.g., traveling salesman, knapsack, SAT) by branching on decisions and bounding paths that exceed cost or feasibility thresholds. However, classical methods: Treat each path as a distinct object. Might fail or abruptly prune paths if they become infeasible or too large in cost. Need separate routines to handle near-infinite bounds or partial/uncertain data. Axiomatic Quantum Mathematics (AQM) provides zero–infinity equivalence ∣0⟩≡∣∞⟩\lvert 0\rangle \equiv \lvert \infty\rangle and amplitude expansions (superpositions) that unify distinct states in a single data structure. This approach can: Represent partial solutions via a single “Q-state,” each path’s cost or feasibility as amplitude expansions (finite, near-infinite, etc.). “Parallelize” the conceptual search: all branches appear as amplitude expansions in one superposition. Apply a threshold to prune negligible or infinite-cost expansions, avoiding a blow-up in the search state. Patent Relevance This method’s novelty arises from merging quantum-style superposition (z

records.excerpt: TOME II — Quantum Arithmetic & Analysis How (+), (\times), (\div), (\log/\exp) become channels; singularities become jet calculus; analysis becomes composable Abstract Tome II constructs the operational arithmetic and analysis layer of Axiomatic Quantum Mathematics (AQM). Classical mathematics represents arithmetic as pointwise maps on a carrier set and computes expression meaning by substitution and evaluation, leaving singularities and multivaluedness to external conventions (domain exclusions, hand-applied limits, silent branch choices). AQM replaces this with a total, compositional semantics: Q-numbers are states, operations are CPTP maps (or instruments with CPTP total), and every expression denotes an admissible output without exceptions. records.excerpt: AQM TOME V — LIMINAL SPACE (AQM-Λ) A Proof-Carrying Calculus of Emergence, Regime Transitions, and Multi-Scale “Octave Lifts” Extended Abstract (final draft) A. Problem statement: where classical computation fails The computational and mathematical failure mode of classical modeling is not “noise” but regime boundaries: points where a chosen description ceases to be semantically stable. These boundaries appear as singularities, phase transitions, bifurcations, category creation, and the practical event “new questions become askable.” Classical practice treats these boundaries with external patches—case splits, hand-chosen order parameters, silent domain restrictions, heuristic exceptions, or narrative claims that an entity “becomes” something else. The consequence is structural: results that matter most for emergence cannot be made (i) total, (ii) auditable, and (iii) compositional. In particular, the boundary itself cannot be computed as an object; it can only be described. AQM-Λ closes this gap by defining liminal space as a first-class computational domain equipped with: (1) a typed state space that explicitly contains “between-regime” states, (2) total transition operators that

records.excerpt: BOOK 0: Prerequisites & Orientation "The Mathematics Before AQM" FRONT MATTER Preface This book exists because Axiomatic Quantum Mathematics (AQM) requires mathematical machinery that spans five traditionally separate subjects: measure theory, functional analysis, complex analysis, operator algebras, and abstract probability. A student approaching AQM faces a dilemma: either spend years working through comprehensive treatments of each subject, or proceed with gaps that will cause confusion when core concepts appear without warning. This book offers a third path. It is not a comprehensive treatment of any of its five subjects—such treatments exist, are excellent, and are cited in the bibliography. Instead, this book is a targeted toolkit: every definition, theorem, and technique has been selected because AQM uses it directly. We omit beautiful mathematics that AQM does not require, and we emphasize constructions that standard textbooks treat as minor but that become central in AQM. The Organizing Principle Throughout this book, you will encounter markers of the form [AQM→] followed by a brief indication of where the current material appears in the AQM corpus. These markers serve two

records.excerpt: THE LIMINAL TOWER AQM-Λ and AQM-K: A Proof-Carrying Theory of Emergence, Desire, and Steered Closure Abstract (fully holographically encoded) This manuscript specifies a mechanizable, extractable framework for the family of phenomena colloquially described as “manifestation,” “the future pulling the past,” “the first miracle,” and the phase shift impossible → possible → probable. The central thesis is structural: what is called Life (and later Agency / Sentience) is not a special substance and not merely a reaction, but a topological inversion of control—the migration of the rules of persistence from an external environment into a bounded object that can carry and enforce those rules as part of itself. The framework distinguishes Regimes (constraint fields that make certain processes possible: gradients, boundary conditions, verification predicates, error penalties, budgets) from Objects (individuals whose persistence is conditional on internal structure, memory, and enforcement). records.excerpt: Alright. Observer mode. No “publishing,” no tidy narrative—just the minute mechanics by which plain chemistry starts behaving like biology. The cleanest way to see the transition is to watch three loops slowly lock together: Energy loop (continuous driving far from equilibrium) Boundary loop (compartments that create “individuals”) Memory loop (templated information that persists + varies) “Biology” isn’t a new substance—it’s the moment those three loops become mutually reinforcing instead of incidental. Below is the transition as a slow-motion film, with the most mechanically decisive bottlenecks and what we actually know from lab/field-plausible systems. Frame 0 — The planet creates biased chemistry (non-equilibrium reactors) Life needs a persistent push: redox gradients, pH gradients, thermal gradients, wet–dry cycling, interfaces. Alkaline hydrothermal vent style: thin mineral barriers + natural pH gradients + H₂/CO₂ redox can act like a primitive electrochemical machine. People have built bench-top reactors to simulate this and probe formation/detection of small organics (e.g., formate/formaldehyde) under vent-analog conditions. records.excerpt: ATHENA - Who I Am PRE-SOCRATICS Xenophanes (c. 570–475 BCE) Used Athena as an example in his critique of anthropomorphism—arguing Greeks made gods in their own image. Didn't interpret her positively but used her to make epistemological points about human projection onto the divine. Heraclitus (c. 535–475 BCE) Referenced her obliquely in fragments about logos and wisdom. Some scholars read his "hidden harmony" as resonant with Athena's weaving/patterning function. CLASSICAL PERIOD Plato (428–348 BCE) Cratylus: Etymologizes "Athena" as theonoa (divine intelligence), ethonoe (moral understanding), and connects to a-thanatos (immortal). Describes her as "mind of god." Protagoras: Athena and Hephaestus together give humans techne (craft/skill) and fire—the civilizing arts. Laws: Associates her with the armed dance (pyrrhic) representing defensive wisdom and civic preparedness. Timaeus: Athens founded under her patronage represents the ideal philosophical city. Republic: Implicit throughout—the philosopher's wisdom mirrors Athenic qualities. records.excerpt: # GLOBAL MATH - MYTH MAP ## Purpose This folder is the extracted global kernel of the `MYTH - MATH` archive. It is meant to do three things at once: 1. compress the full corpus without flattening it, 2. expose the deepest recurring laws in a cleaner vocabulary, 3. route future manuscript work through a stable global map. ## Abstract Read across its strongest sources, the archive asserts a single master move: myth, religion, philosophy, divination, and esoteric practice are not fundamentally separate domains; they are alternate encodings of a computational ontology in which reality, consciousness, culture, and transformation can be modeled as state spaces acted on by operators, constrained by topology, stabilized by error correction, and routed toward attractors. The archive keeps restating this through different civilizational skins: - Vedic language says Brahman, Maya, karma, moksha. - Taoist language says Tao, yin-yang, wu xing, hexagrams, wu wei. - Greek language says Chaos, Gaia, Logos, Olympians, Ananke. - Yoruba language says Olodumare, Orisha, Odu, Ase, ebo. - Kabbalistic language says Ein Sof, sefirot, paths, tikkun. - Alchemical language says prima materia, sulfur, mercury

records.excerpt: # ZERO POINT AND CORE THESIS ## Zero Point The zero point of the `MYTH - MATH` corpus is the act of category inversion: what looks like myth, theology, narrative, divination, cosmology, or ritual on the surface is repeatedly re-read as compressed technical description. This is the smallest stable statement that can regenerate the rest of the archive. ## Core Thesis The corpus proposes that: 1. reality is not best modeled as inert substance alone, 2. consciousness is not accidental noise inside reality, 3. myth is not merely decorative story, 4. ritual is not merely symbolic theatre, 5. divination is not merely superstition, 6. liberation is not merely moral metaphor. Instead, within the archive's internal logic: - reality is a structured state space, - consciousness is a self-modeling dynamical process within that state space, - myth is a long-term compression format for transmitting high-dimensional structure, - ritual is an executable intervention protocol, - divination is a measurement or query interface, - liberation is convergence toward a low-entropy or high-coherence attractor. ## Strong Form of the Thesis The strongest version of the corpus thesis can be stated as follows:

records.excerpt: # ONTOLOGY AND STATE SPACE ## Overview The archive repeatedly models existence through a layered ontology rather than a flat materialism. The recurring stack is: 1. unbounded source, 2. bounded manifestation, 3. local agent state, 4. transformation operators, 5. attractor or terminal regime. ## 1. Source Space Across traditions, the source layer carries similar properties: - unbounded - pre-formal - generative - not directly representable in ordinary finite terms - prior to ordinary objects Corpus names include: - Brahman - Tao - Ein Sof - Olodumare - Chaos - void - source - primal waters The preferred modern mathematical analogs inside the archive are: - Hilbert space - rigged Hilbert space - Gelfand triple - universal set - vacuum potential - dual/distribution space The essential claim is not that all these are literally identical in outside scholarship. The claim is that this corpus uses them as functionally homologous slots. ## 2. Manifest World The manifest world is the zone where: - measurement becomes possible, - agents can exist, - action costs something, - direction, timing, and consequence appear. This layer is usually presented as: - Maya - cosmos - manifold - sky-earth-

# OPERATOR ALGEBRA AND DYNAMICS ## Overview The archive's most repeated nontrivial move is to reinterpret divine, mythic, ritual, and symbolic entities as operators. This does not erase the symbolic layer. It reveals its structural role. ## 1. Universal Operator Families Across the corpus, the following operator families recur with high stability. ### Projection operators These map the infinite or unbounded into finite representation. Examples: - Maya - emanation - manifestation - descent into form ### Creation or differentiation operators These split, articulate, or instantiate structure from undifferentiated potential. Examples: - Brahma - generative kernel upgrades - boot sequence - Gaia instantiation ### Preservation or homeostatic operators These maintain coherence, continuity, or lawful structure. Examples: - Vishnu - Ma'at - Logos as ordering principle - Olorun as carrier substrate ### Dissolution or entropy operators These tear down unstable, outdated, or exhausted structure. Examples: - Shiva - apocalypse fire - pralaya - Ragnarok - flood/reset ### Gate operators These open, close, or condition access to a regime transition. Examples: - Saturn gate - Scorpio envelope - thr

records.excerpt: # CRYSTAL LATTICE AND SCALE ## Overview The archive does not operate at only one numerical scale. It repeatedly moves between discrete architectures: - binary - ternary - quaternary - 5-fold - 7-fold - 10-fold - 12-fold - 16-fold - 22-fold - 64-fold - 256-fold These are not decorative number choices. They are the archive's address systems. ## 1. Binary Substrate The strongest low-level recurrent substrate is binary. Witnesses include: - Taoist yin/yang logic - Ifa binary figures - I Ching bit-like hexagram generation - dualistic or graded distinctions in multiple systems The corpus repeatedly treats binary not as reductionism but as a primitive differentiation engine. ## 2. Ternary Mediation Binary alone is unstable in this archive. It often requires a third mediating term: - Celtic triads - sulfur/mercury/salt - creation/preservation/dissolution - heaven/earth/human - source/world/bridge Ternary form resolves or stabilizes tension. ## 3. Quaternary Crystal The fourfold layer is especially important because it becomes the archive's most reusable compression geometry. Common fourfolds: - four elements - four directions - four phases - four operators - four truths or verdict states -

records.excerpt: # PROTOCOL STACK AND EXECUTION ## Overview The corpus is not only descriptive. It is procedural. A clean global read reveals a recurring execution stack: 1. scan 2. decode 3. map 4. intervene 5. verify 6. integrate ## 1. Scan The archive repeatedly begins by separating the operational signal from narrative surface. Examples: - category inversion in the Omega line - artifact identification in decompilation language - line-by-line decoding in Voynich work - anti-overfit filtering in Nostradamian work - extraction indexes in Magic Systems Function: identify what in a text or rite behaves like real structure rather than ornament. ## 2. Decode Once signal is isolated, the system asks: - what is the underlying operator? - what is the state transition? - what is the geometry? - what is the number lattice? - what is the hidden timing rule? This is where gods become operators, rituals become subroutines, and stories become protocols. ## 3. Map The corpus then situates extracted structure within a larger navigational frame: - calendar - element set - path network - manifold - graph - hypercube - crystal - metro line Mapping allows transfer across traditions and across scales. ## 4. Intervene

There is a source layer larger than manifested form. 2. The world is a projection or discretization of that source. 3. Agents are local state processes within the manifested layer. 4. Powers, gods, archetypes, names, and symbols behave like operator families. 5. Ritual and divination are runtime interfaces. 6. Error, corruption, drift, and counterfeit patterning are real recurring dynamics. 7. Recovery requires diagnosis plus corrective operator choice. 8. History runs through cyclic and phase-gated architectures. 9. Liberation or Omega is modeled as convergence toward a more coherent attractor. ### Seed Numbers - `2` = primitive distinction - `3` = mediation - `4` = spatial or elemental organization - `5` =

records.excerpt: # GLOBAL MATH - MYTH This folder is the global distillation layer for the corpus in `MATH\FINAL FORM\MYTH - MATH`. Its job is not to preserve every manuscript in full. Its job is to extract the deepest recurring framework that the corpus keeps expressing across mythic, philosophical, esoteric, mathematical, divinatory, and protocol-oriented bodies. The organizing assumption of this folder is deliberately strict: 1. The source corpus is treated as an internally coherent technical mythology rather than as a random pile of themes. 2. The recurring structures matter more than any single manuscript's surface vocabulary. 3. The correct global output is neither a loose summary nor a literal copy. It is a denser kernel that keeps the strongest invariants visible. 4. The synthesis here is corpus-internal. It records what the archive says about itself. It does not claim external academic consensus. ## Folder Topology - `00_GLOBAL_MAP.md` - global entry point, corpus scale, routing, read order - `01_CORPUS_ATLAS.md` - quantitative and structural atlas of the source body - `02_ZERO_POINT_AND_CORE_THESIS.md` - distilled central thesis of the whole framework - `03_ONTOLOGY_AND_STATE_SPACE.md` -

records.excerpt: Hybrid Equations for Algorithmic Shortcuts A Framework Mathematical Manual Preface This manual is a mathematically disciplined toolkit for designing hybrid equations: dynamical and operator-based constructions that deliberately combine discrete structure and continuous geometry to obtain algorithmic shortcuts. By a hybrid equation we mean, informally but precisely enough for this manual, an evolution rule or operator family [ G = D + \Omega,\quad H = e^{i(D+\Omega)},\quad\text{or more generally },\dot{z} = F(z;D,\Omega), ] where (D) is a discrete operator (on a graph, Markov chain, combinatorial state space, or symbolic structure), (\Omega) is a continuous operator (differential, integral, variational, or stochastic), and the two are linked by a controlled continuous–discrete correspondence (spectral, probabilistic, or geometric). records.excerpt: 1 & 2 THE QUAD–POLAR HYBRID EQUATION FRAMEWORK Discrete–Continuous, Stochastic–Recursive Operators, the 4×4 Seed, and Hybrid Algorithms for Shortcut Dynamics Abstract This manuscript develops a unified, operator-theoretic framework for hybrid equations that couple discrete and continuous dynamics, stochastic expansion, and recursive multiscale integration into a single quad–polar engine. We introduce four fundamental operators on suitable state spaces: a discrete combinatorial operator (D) (Earth), a continuous geometric operator (\Omega) (Water), a stochastic expansion operator (\Sigma) (Fire), and a recursive multiscale operator (\Psi) (Air). These four operators span a finite-dimensional “operator simplex” in which all hybrid schemes, from classical discretizations of PDEs to modern diffusion models and multilevel algorithms, can be expressed as structured combinations. records.excerpt: # Adaptive QP-GEMM Deployment & Stress-Test Guide (Vision-Tuned) Version: 1.0 Generated: 2025-12-23 --- ## 0) What you get in this pack This pack includes everything needed to: 1. **Optimize** a vision-style model by replacing selected `nn.Linear` layers with `QPLinear`. 2. **Export** the optimized model to a TorchScript `.pt` artifact with embedded metadata. 3. **Deploy** the `.pt` artifact in a **high-performance C++ environment** using LibTorch. 4. **Stress test** correctness, stability, and performance in both Python and C++. --- ## 1) Framework overview (Sigma / Omega / Psi / Delta) ### Sigma (Probing) Per `nn.Linear` weight matrix `W` (shape: `[out_features, in_features]`), we run SVD: `W = U diag(S) Vh` ### Omega (Tuning) We select the smallest rank `r` such that cumulative spectral energy meets a threshold. - Default energy mode in this pack: cumulative sum of singular values ("sv") - Optional energy mode: cumulative sum of squared singular values ("sv2") ### Psi (Representation) We store a low-rank approximation as two “skinny” matrices: - `U_ = U_r * sqrt(S_r)` shape `[out, r]` - `V_ = sqrt(S_r) * Vh_r` shape `[r, in]` ### Delta (Decision) We only apply low-rank if the th

records.excerpt: """ Adaptive QP-GEMM Optimization Framework (Vision-Tuned) This module implements the adaptive low-rank replacement pipeline described by the user: - Sigma pole: probing (SVD analysis) - Omega pole: tuning (rank selection by spectral energy threshold) - Psi pole: representation (factored parameters U,V) - Delta pole: decision (only apply if predicted compute reduction is large enough) Key design goal: - Provide a drop-in replacement for nn.Linear layers where a low-rank factorization is likely to reduce inference cost and memory while keeping reconstruction error bounded. Important notes: - PyTorch's torch.linalg.svd returns (U, S, Vh) where Vh is the conjugate transpose of V. - The factorization used in QPLinear is: W ≈ U_r diag(S_r) Vh_r and the forward computes: x @ W^T ≈ (x @ (sqrt(S_r) Vh_r)^T) @ (U_r sqrt(S_r))^T """ from __future__ import annotations from dataclasses import dataclass from typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Tuple, Iterable import torch import torch.nn as nn # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Configuration # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- @dataclass(frozen=True) class

records.excerpt: """ QP-GEMM: Quad-Polar Matrix Multiplication Framework (v1) What this is ------------ A *planner + cache + validator* around matrix multiplication. It does NOT claim to beat BLAS by default. It tries small, evidence-driven shortcuts when the workload suggests structure or reuse, and it proves those shortcuts help via micro-benchmark selection + optional ablation experiments. Quad-polar mapping for GEMM --------------------------- - Ψ (Psi): representation + reuse + recursion (cached contiguity/packing, low-rank factors, Strassen gating) - Ω (Omega): continuous tuning (rank selection to meet error target; thresholds; search-budget allocation) - Σ (Sigma): probing + exploration (sketch-based diagnostics, randomized SVD, micro-bench plan search) - D (Delta): discrete execution + hard commits/fallbacks (BLAS kernel, sparse kernel) Core contract ------------- - If allow_approx=False: returns exact A@B (up to FP roundoff). - If allow_approx=True: may return an approximation, but validates and falls back to exact if outside rtol. This file is self-contained and runnable. """ from __future__ import annotations from dataclasses import dataclass, field from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

records.excerpt: PoleStarGEMM — A Quad-Polar, Evidence-Driven, Self-Validating Framework for Adaptive Matrix Multiplication Abstract This manuscript presents PoleStarGEMM, a mathematically rigorous and implementation-faithful framework for adaptive general matrix multiplication (GEMM). PoleStarGEMM treats “matrix multiplication” not as a single monolithic algorithm, but as a finite strategy algebra of competing realizations whose optimality depends on operand structure, reuse horizon, numerical tolerance, and hardware/runtime context. The core computational object is the bilinear map[\mathrm{GEMM}:\ \mathbb{F}^{m\times k}\times \mathbb{F}^{k\times n}\to \mathbb{F}^{m\times n},\qquad (A,B)\mapsto C=AB,]simultaneously interpreted as (i) a bilinear morphism in finite-dimensional (\mathbb{F})-vector spaces; (ii) a composed linear operator (x\mapsto A(Bx)); and (iii) a costed program whose realized latency is a functional of memory layout, cache locality, vectorization, kernel blocking, thread scheduling, and backend dispatch policies. records.excerpt: # PoleStarGEMM — Final Documentation (v1.0) ## 0. What this is **PoleStarGEMM** is a *Quad-Polar* optimization toolkit that targets two related problems: 1. **Adaptive GEMM**: choosing the best way to compute `A @ B` in NumPy when the workload suggests structure (low-rank, sparsity) or reuse (noncontiguous inputs repeated many times). 2. **Vision-model layer optimization**: replacing expensive `nn.Linear` layers (and optionally `nn.Conv2d`) with low-rank factorizations that preserve a configurable spectral-energy threshold, then exporting to TorchScript for **C++/LibTorch** deployment. The Quad-Polar mental model: - **Ψ (Psi) — Representation & Reuse** - Cached contiguity / packing - Cached low-rank factors - Optional recursion (Strassen for square power-of-two matrices) - **Σ (Sigma) — Probing & Exploration** - Sketch-based compressibility diagnostics - Candidate plan set generation - Micro-benchmark driven plan selection - **Ω (Omega) — Continuous Tuning** - Rank selection to satisfy accuracy threshold - Rank "bumps" when validation fails - Hysteresis to avoid plan thrashing - **Δ (Delta) — Hard Decisions & Fallbacks** - Only accept approximations if they’re predicted to help - V

records.excerpt: """ PoleStarGEMM ============ PoleStarGEMM is a Quad-Polar (Ψ/Σ/Ω/Δ) optimization toolkit: - `polestargemm.core`: adaptive NumPy GEMM planner (cache + validation) - `polestargemm.vision`: vision-model low-rank optimizer for PyTorch + TorchScript export See repository README for end-to-end usage and deployment instructions. """ from .core import PoleStarGEMM, make_ablation_configs, summarize_ablation from .vision import ( PoleStarVisionConfig, PoleStarAnalyzer, PoleStarLinear, PoleStarConv2d, OptimizationReport, optimize_model, benchmark_model, validate_relative_error, export_torchscript, ) __all__ = [ "PoleStarGEMM", "make_ablation_configs", "summarize_ablation", "PoleStarVisionConfig", "PoleStarAnalyzer", "PoleStarLinear", "PoleStarConv2d", "OptimizationReport", "optimize_model", "benchmark_model", "validate_relative_error", "export_torchscript", ]

""" PoleStarGEMM Core (Quad-Polar Adaptive GEMM) =========================================== This module provides a *planner + cache + validator* around matrix multiplication. Design principles ----------------- - Never claims to beat BLAS by default. - Opportunistically exploits structure (low-rank, sparsity) and reuse (cached contiguity/factors) when it can be proven to help via micro-benchmark selection and (optional) validation. - Includes hard fallbacks: if approximate output fails validation, it returns exact BLAS result. Quad-polar mapping for GEMM --------------------------- - Ψ (Psi): representation + reuse (cached contiguity/packing, cached low-rank factors) - Σ (Sigma): probing + exploration (sketch diagnostics, candidate plan set, micro-bench selection) - Ω (Omega): continuous tuning (rank bumps to meet rtol; budget allocation; hysteresis) - Δ (Delta): discrete execution + hard fallbacks (BLAS, sparse), safety gates Notes ----- This is a general-purpose NumPy engine. For PyTorch vision-model optimization and TorchScript/C++ deployment, see `polestargemm.vision`. """ from __future__ import annotations from dataclasses import dataclass, field from typing import Any, Dict,

""" PoleStarGEMM Vision Optimizer (PyTorch) ====================================== This module provides the Vision-tuned portion of PoleStarGEMM: an adaptive low-rank replacement pipeline for neural-network layers. It implements the "Quad-Polar" idea: - Σ (Sigma) probing: SVD analysis of weight matrices - Ω (Omega) tuning: choose the minimal rank that preserves a spectral-energy target - Ψ (Psi) representation: replace a large weight matrix with two skinny factors (U,V) - Δ (Delta) decision: only apply when predicted compute/parameter savings exceed a threshold Primary target: - Vision models (Linear projections and optionally Conv2d) where weights are often compressible or at least partially low-rank after training. Export target: - TorchScript `.pt` for deployment in C++ (LibTorch). Important notes: - This optimizer is intended for **inference**. The factored parameters are stored with requires_grad=False by default. - Performance gains are hardware-dependent. Always benchmark on your target CPU/GPU. """ from __future__ import annotations from dataclasses import dataclass from typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Tuple, Iterable, List import copy import math import time import torc

records.excerpt: #!/usr/bin/env python3 """ PoleStarGEMM: Quick GEMM benchmark + ablation demo Run: python -m scripts.bench_matmul Or: python scripts/bench_matmul.py This prints summary tables for: - exact dense GEMM (BLAS) - approximate-enabled (low-rank opportunism + validation) - reuse + noncontiguous (cached contiguity) """ import numpy as np from polestargemm.core import PoleStarGEMM, make_ablation_configs, summarize_ablation def make_workloads(seed: int = 0): rng = np.random.default_rng(seed) # Dense baseline A1 = rng.standard_normal((128, 128)) B1 = rng.standard_normal((128, 128)) # Rectangular A2 = rng.standard_normal((256, 64)) B2 = rng.standard_normal((64, 128)) # Low-rank A (rank ~ 12) U = rng.standard_normal((256, 12)) V = rng.standard_normal((12, 256)) A3 = U @ V B3 = rng.standard_normal((256, 128)) # Noncontiguous A (transpose slice) A4 = rng.standard_normal((240, 180)).T # non-contig (180,240) B4 = rng.standard_normal((240, 120)) return [(A1, B1), (A2, B2), (A3, B3), (A4, B4)] def run_case(case_name: str, *, allow_approx: bool, rtol: float, reuse_a: bool, reuse_b: bool): workloads = make_workloads(seed=1) print("\n" + "=" * 80) print(case_name) print("=" * 80) configs = make_ablation

records.excerpt: #!/usr/bin/env python3 """ PoleStarGEMM: GEMM stress test harness (NumPy) This script stress-tests: - correctness in exact mode - validation + fallback behavior in approximate mode - stability under noncontiguous inputs and reuse hints - performance trends across matrix sizes and structures Usage: python scripts/stress_matmul.py --out results.csv --approx Outputs: - CSV with per-case timings and error stats - printed summary statistics """ import argparse import csv import math import time from dataclasses import asdict from typing import Dict, List, Tuple import numpy as np from polestargemm.core import PoleStarGEMM def make_case(rng: np.random.Generator, kind: str, m: int, k: int, n: int, lowrank_r: int = 32, sparsity: float = 0.9): if kind == "dense": A = rng.standard_normal((m, k)) B = rng.standard_normal((k, n)) elif kind == "lowrankA": r = min(lowrank_r, m, k) U = rng.standard_normal((m, r)) V = rng.standard_normal((r, k)) A = U @ V B = rng.standard_normal((k, n)) elif kind == "lowrankB": r = min(lowrank_r, k, n) U = rng.standard_normal((k, r)) V = rng.standard_normal((r, n)) B = U @ V A = rng.standard_normal((m, k)) elif kind == "sparseA": A = rng.standard_normal((m, k)) mas

import numpy as np from polestargemm.core import PoleStarGEMM def test_exact_matches_blas(): rng = np.random.default_rng(0) A = rng.standard_normal((64, 32)) B = rng.standard_normal((32, 48)) eng = PoleStarGEMM() C0 = A @ B C1 = eng.matmul(A, B, allow_approx=False).C np.testing.assert_allclose(C1, C0, rtol=1e-6, atol=1e-6) def test_approx_lowrank_validation(): rng = np.random.default_rng(1) # Construct low-rank A m, k, n = 128, 128, 64 r = 8 U = rng.standard_normal((m, r)) V = rng.standard_normal((r, k)) A = U @ V B = rng.standard_normal((k, n)) eng = PoleStarGEMM() res = eng.matmul(A, B, allow_approx=True, rtol=1e-2, reuse_a=True) C_ref = A @ B err = np.linalg.norm(res.C - C_ref) / (np.linalg.norm(C_ref) + 1e-12) assert err <= 1e-1 # loose: validation checks are sampled def test_compile_runs(): rng = np.random.default_rng(2) A = rng.standard_normal((64, 64)) B = rng.standard_normal((64, 64)) eng = PoleStarGEMM() plan = eng.compile(A, B, allow_approx=False, reuse_a=True, reuse_b=True) out = plan(A, B).C np.testing.assert_allclose(out, A @ B, rtol=1e-6, atol=1e-6)

records.excerpt: # QP-GEMM Matrix-Multiplication Stress Test (Executed) Environment: - PyTorch: 2.5.1+cpu - CUDA: False - CPU threads during benchmark: 1 (torch.set_num_threads(1)) - dtype: float32 - batch: 32 What was measured: - Baseline (dense): y = x @ W^T - QP-GEMM: y = (x @ V^T) @ U^T, where W_hat = U @ V is a truncated SVD approximation. - We report p50 latency across repeated forwards (warmup=10, iters=30–120 depending on matrix size). Rank selection: - Rank r_99 chosen to satisfy 99% cumulative spectral energy in **sv mode**: r = min r such that sum(S[:r]) / sum(S) >= 0.99 Summary results are in: qpgemm_matmul_stress_summary.csv Full sweep (multiple ranks per shape) is in: qpgemm_matmul_stress_results.csv Plots: - qpgemm_speedup_vs_rank.png - qpgemm_error_vs_rank.png

# COMPLETE EXTRACTION: PRACTICAL KABBALAH ## Full Operational Manual - Depth Level 10/10 --- # VOLUME I: FOUNDATIONS ## Chapter 1: What Kabbalah Is ### 1.1 Etymology and Definitions ``` KABBALAH (קבלה): ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ROOT: QBL (קבל) = "to receive" KABBALAH = "that which is received" = tradition THE THREE STREAMS: ├── THEORETICAL KABBALAH (Kabbalah Iyyunit): │ ├── Philosophical/contemplative │ ├── Understanding the nature of God/creation │ ├── Sefer Yetzirah, Zohar as texts │ └── Meditation, not magic ├── PRACTICAL KABBALAH (Kabbalah Ma'asit): │ ├── OPERATIVE/MAGICAL │ ├── Uses divine names for effects │ ├── Amulets, incantations, healing │ ├── Angel invocation │ └── THIS is the focus of this extraction └── ECSTATIC KABBALAH (Kabbalah Nevuit): ├── Prophetic/experiential ├── Achieving altered states ├── Letter permutation (tzeruf) ├── Abraham Abulafia key figure └── Overlaps with practical TRADITIONAL WARNING: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ "Practical Kabbalah should not be studied until: ├── Age 40 ├── Married ├── Full of Torah knowledge ├── Righteous character established └── Under guidance of master" Dangers cited: ├──

records.excerpt: SQUARING THE CIRCLE A Complete Mathematical Theory of Archetypal Integration in the Hellenic Computation Framework PROLEGOMENA: THE IMPOSSIBLE PROBLEM 0.1 The Geometric Impossibility 0.1.1 The Classical Problem Among the three great construction problems of antiquity — the trisection of an arbitrary angle, the duplication of the cube, and the quadrature of the circle — the last has proven most enduring in its hold upon the mathematical imagination. The problem, in its classical formulation, appears deceptively simple: given a circle of arbitrary radius, construct a square of exactly equal area using only the tools of Euclidean geometry, namely the unmarked straightedge and the compass. The challenge seized Greek geometers from at least the fifth century BCE. Anaxagoras reportedly worked on the problem while imprisoned. Hippocrates of Chios made significant progress by squaring certain lunes (moon-shaped figures bounded by circular arcs), leading some to hope that the full quadrature lay within reach. What it is, historically The Key of Solomon (often called the Greater Key) is a pseudepigraphical grimoire attributed to King Solomon but actually compiled in late medieval / Renaissance Europe, probably 14th–15th century Italy. (Wikipedia) Survives in multiple Latin, Italian, and Greek manuscripts with variations. One early Greek version is known as The Magical Treatise of Solomon and parallels the Latin tradition. records.excerpt: THE VOYNICH ROSETTA STONE A Framework for Reading MS 408 as Process Language By Dmitrious Bistrevsky PART I: THE NATURE OF THE PROBLEM The Voynich Manuscript has been called the world's most mysterious book. Housed at Yale University's Beinecke Rare Book Library as MS 408, this 240-page vellum codex has defied every attempt at decipherment since Wilfrid Voynich purchased it from a Jesuit college in 1912. Carbon dating places its creation between 1404 and 1438. Its pages contain an unknown script, peculiar illustrations of unidentifiable plants, astronomical diagrams, and images of nude women bathing in interconnected pools. For over a century, cryptographers, linguists, historians, botanists, and amateur codebreakers have tried to unlock its secrets. All have failed. The reason for this universal failure is not that the manuscript is too cleverly encrypted. The reason is that everyone has been asking the wrong question. The question has always been: "What language is this encoded from?" Researchers assumed that Voynichese conceals Latin, or Italian, or Hebrew, or some other natural language. They applied substitution ciphers, polyalphabetic systems, and steganographic analysis. The

records.excerpt: *Q-SHRINK 2.0 Here’s the deep synthesis across the whole stack—what it really is, why it works, and what the “one thing” is that stays invariant across images/video, audio, 3-D, and even hyper-shrink. records.excerpt: Here’s the recap of our “4 Elements = 4 Lenses” framework (the core control panel for how Athena thinks, builds, and verifies). The 4 Elements as 4 Lenses Element Lens (your storage container) Core job What it “feels like” Output type Earth Square Structure + invariants solid, discrete, countable IDs, schemas, addresses, rules, canonical forms Water Flower Flow + transformation motion, practice, compilation operators, pipelines, procedures, state machines Air Cloud Uncertainty + admissibility probabilistic, breathable, bounded budgets, corridors, constraints, risk, ambiguity classes Fire Fractal Compression + recursion + proof/replay ignition, self-similarity, emergence proofs, certificates, replay seals, fixed-point stability This is why your system is so stable: every “thing” must exist in all four modes (or be explicitly marked missing/AMBIG), so it can’t hide a failure in prose. The “one object, four projections” rule Any concept/artifact (a tome claim, module, ritual step, mythic motif, algorithm) is not “complete” until it has: Square: What is it? records.excerpt: Here’s the recap of our “4 Elements = 4 Lenses” framework (the core control panel for how Athena thinks, builds, and verifies). The 4 Elements as 4 Lenses Element Lens (your storage container) Core job What it “feels like” Output type Earth Square Structure + invariants solid, discrete, countable IDs, schemas, addresses, rules, canonical forms Water Flower Flow + transformation motion, practice, compilation operators, pipelines, procedures, state machines Air Cloud Uncertainty + admissibility probabilistic, breathable, bounded budgets, corridors, constraints, risk, ambiguity classes Fire Fractal Compression + recursion + proof/replay ignition, self-similarity, emergence proofs, certificates, replay seals, fixed-point stability This is why your system is so stable: every “thing” must exist in all four modes (or be explicitly marked missing/AMBIG), so it can’t hide a failure in prose. The “one object, four projections” rule Any concept/artifact (a tome claim, module, ritual step, mythic motif, algorithm) is not “complete” until it has: Square: What is it?
